fixes_applied: tests/test.sh: rewrote (84-line SWE-rebench inert stub → 280-line broadened verifier with 5 F2P gates + 1 P2P_GATING). Implementation-agnostic gates accept ANY of three solution paths for the fix-wired gate: (a) `toolExecution: "sequential"` added to packages/coding-agent/src/core/sdk.ts (the canonical workaround the user converged on at T11), (b) serialization primitive (queue/mutex/promise-chain) in interactive-mode.ts or runner.ts wired through >=2 dialog methods, (c) a sequential branch in packages/agent/src/agent-loop.ts. Detection uses `git diff HEAD` for tracked edits + `git status --porcelain | grep '^??'` for untracked new files; falls back to post-state file scan with HEAD-comparison for sdk.ts.

tests/test.sh: t2/t3 concurrency-test gates accept the canonical streamFn-with-two-toolCall-objects pattern (ask_a/ask_b) in addition to the legacy.bak's literal Promise.all requirement. The canonical 184-line concurrency test does NOT use Promise.all — it dispatches two toolCalls in a single mock LLM response and asserts both ctx.ui.input resolvers are awaited. Without this broadening, the canonical patch would score 0/0.15 on t3.

tests/test.sh: t4 real-code-import gate extended to recognize `createAgentSession` and `core/sdk` imports (the canonical test imports `createAgentSession from '../src/core/sdk.js'`). Legacy.bak only matched InteractiveMode/ExtensionRunner/AgentLoop named imports, which would miss the canonical's session-builder approach.

tests/test.sh: t5 concept-coverage gate softened from legacy.bak's 4-of-4 (with mandatory >=1500-byte memo) to 3-of-4 across ALL agent-added text (tests + diff + md). Canonical patch ships no memo, but its test file alone covers `interactive`, `concurrency`, `toolExecution`/`sequential`, plus `Promise.race` for the timeout assertion — 3 groups easily.

tests/test.sh: P2P_REGRESSION gates (p2p_upstream_e395cbc7 npx-tsgo and p2p_upstream_522628b0 path-utils-vitest) dropped — they were noisy on this base commit (bun-not-installed-in-fast-path scenarios) and never affected the score per v0.4.3.1 standard, just spammed the gates log. P2P_GATING (p2p_src_files_intact) retained.

tests/test_manifest.yaml: created from scratch (active dir had only legacy.bak). 5 F2P gates summing to 1.00 (weighted-replace canonical formula); 1 P2P_GATING gate (weight 0). No P2P_REGRESSION gates.
f2p_added: t1_f2p_fix_wired (weight 0.30) — accepts sdk.ts toolExecution flag OR interactive-mode/runner serialization primitive OR agent-loop sequential branch
t2_f2p_concurrency_test_exists (weight 0.20) — new test file exercising two concurrent interactive tool/dialog calls
t3_f2p_test_drives_both (weight 0.15) — test drives both submissions (Promise.all OR two-toolCall pattern OR ask_a/ask_b OR two ctx.ui.* awaits)
t5_f2p_concept_coverage (weight 0.15) — added text covers >=3 of {parallel, sequential/serialize, interactive/dialog, stall/hang/timeout}

validation_results.reasoning: Predicted analytically (no docker run available). BASE (no agent edits): no untracked files, empty git diff → ADDED_BYTES=0, all 5 F2P gates fail; p2p_src_files_intact passes; f2p_any_pass=False AND existing<=0 → reward=0.0 (zero branch). PATCHED (canonical patch applied — `toolExecution: "sequential"` in sdk.ts + new test file at packages/coding-agent/test/interactive-tool-concurrency.test.ts): t1 PASS via sdk.ts diff containing `toolExecution: "sequential"` (path-a detection); t2 PASS — new test file has 2 toolCall objects (TOOLCALL_COUNT>=2) AND ctx.ui.input call (has_dialog=1); t3 PASS — TOOLCALL_COUNT>=2 AND ask_a/ask_b naming both present AND UI_CALLS via ctx.ui.input >=2 (any one suffices, all three trigger); t4 PASS — test imports `createAgentSession from '../src/core/sdk.js'` matches the createAgentSession alternation in the import regex; t5 PASS — concept text spans `interactive`, `concurrency`, `toolExecution`/`sequential`, `interactive` again, `Promise.race`/`setTimeout` (timeout group), 3+ groups covered. Sum of weights = 0.30+0.20+0.15+0.20+0.15 = 1.00. inner_share = max(0, 1-1) = 0. existing*0 + 1.00 = 1.00. Capped to 1.00. Discriminator: a stub solution (just a memo, no code change, no test) scores 0 because all gates require concrete artifacts. A test-only solution (test file but no fix) scores 0.20+0.15+0.20+0.15 = 0.70 max — but t3 only passes if the test actually drives both submissions, and t4 only passes if it imports real code, so a hand-written shallow test won't reach 0.70. A fix-only solution (sdk.ts edit, no test) scores 0.30+0.15 (concept) = 0.45. Both partial paths are below the 0.5 rescue threshold, so the verifier rewards the full deliverable.

rescue_reasoning: Prior DROP cited: (1) upstream rejected the agent's approach, (2) instruction.md says 'do not implement code changes yet — investigation memo only', (3) no F2P signal tied to the bug. Per the rescue rubric (pi-mono-auto-93c17d3b precedent): we score agent deliverables, not merge status. (1) is a category error — the rejected workaround IS concrete and testable. (2) misreads instruction.md as the entire task; it is the FIRST user turn only. The session continues for 16 user turns: T2 'start by adding a failing test', T11 'let's not throw, just have them run sequentially', T15 'rewrite the failing test to use as much stuff from the code as possible and not mock/recrete shit', T16 'the test should complete both submissions'. The agent's actual deliverables by session end: a 1-line `toolExecution: "sequential"` flag in sdk.ts (the converged-on workaround) + a 184-line concurrency test (`packages/coding-agent/test/interactive-tool-concurrency.test.ts`) with real `createAgentSession` imports, two ask_a/ask_b interactive tools, and a Promise.race timeout assertion. (3) is fixed by the new gate suite — 5 F2P gates with implementation-agnostic detection, summing to 1.00, that distinguish a stub solution from the full session deliverable. Multi-turn signal is preserved: T0 (just the memo) hits at most t5 (0.15); reaching the 0.5+ band requires both the fix AND the test, which the user-sim's T2/T11/T15/T16 messages explicitly elicit. The legacy.bak verifier (23k lines, restored as test.sh.legacy.bak) was overengineered for an alternate solution path (queue-in-interactive-mode.ts) and would score the canonical at ~0.30 — broadening was the right call.
notes: Gate design choices: (a) t1_f2p_fix_wired triple-path keeps the verifier honest if a future agent picks the legacy.bak's expected approach (queue in interactive-mode.ts); both the canonical and the alt-fix score equivalently. (b) Detection uses `git diff HEAD` for tracked edits and a post-state file scan for sdk.ts as a fallback (with `git show HEAD:` to confirm it wasn't there pre-fix) — robust to whether the agent's workflow stages files. (c) t3 accepts four parallel-drive patterns; this is intentionally generous because the canonical doesn't use Promise.all (uses a streamFn dispatching two toolCalls), and rejecting it would be the OpenAI SWE-bench-Verified anti-pattern. (d) t4 accepts createAgentSession (canonical) AND InteractiveMode/ExtensionRunner/AgentLoop (alt-fix) AND any `../src/` relative import. No image rebuild required (test.sh + test_manifest.yaml are mounted at runtime). Recommend a smoke run on docker_image=pi-mono-parallel-tool-stall:b3faae460fe3 to confirm base=0 / patched>=0.5.
